programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Bel and John are building a radical underground house on 25 spectacular acres at Whittlesea, in Victoria's Plenty Valley - so named for its fertile farmland and magnificent views. But the area has also acquired a more terrifying reputation. In 2009, 120 people died nearby at Kinglake in what's now known as the Black Saturday bushfires, and as a result Bel and John want a home that's fire proof! With John away at work for much of the build, it's up to Bel to project manage the difficult and complex build. But a number of unexpected challenges make the process harder than anticipated, and with their underground sanctuary looking more like an airport terminal than a stylish home, the pressure reaches breaking point!

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Rhonda Martin is a colourful woman who has stamped her bright personality all over her waterfront Coogee home. Her long-suffering husband Peter has painstakingly painted the ceiling roses to match the wallpaper but was over the house and Rhonda's decorating ideas years ago. Peter wants to sell up and hit the road in their camper van, however, Rhonda is yet to cut the grandkids apron strings. Two-and-a-half years ago, she agreed to put the house on the market - for $4.5 million. She's slowly coming round to Peter's way of thinking and a more realistic asking price of $3 million. But even with the knockout views, there aren't many buyers who will pay top dollar to live in a kaleidoscope.

programme.title: Jeopardy!

programme.title: In Plain Sight
programme.desc: Leo Billups, formerly known as Lonny McRoy, had the bad fortune of witnessing a drug related murder that involved the Cruz family, one of the most powerful drug families in the country. It's a common enough story in WITSEC but what sets this one apart is that the person he saw murdered was his own mother and Leo is eight years old. When Leo enters WITSEC, no one's really sure what to do with him. His father can't go into the programme, since he's one of the people Leo is testifying against. So of course, Stan McQueen (Mary and Marshall's boss) looks at Mary for the answer. Mary Shannon is not exactly the maternal type, although her caretaking instinct is a lot stronger than she would admit, probably because she doesn't even realise how good she is at taking care of everyone. Mary's bright idea is to ask (or rather strongly persuade) another WITSEC family if they'll adopt him.

programme.title: In Plain Sight
programme.desc: Treena Morris has had more experience taking on new identities than most protected witnesses. Treena, aka Trudy Wainright, aka Tenley Bishop, aka Toni Denard, aka Teri Ranzino is a con artist (even worse than murderers in Mary's opinion). Her particular skill: making men fall so crazy, head-over-heels in love with her that they'll do stupid things, such as hand her a bag full of illegal conflict diamonds and trust that she'll put them in the safe. Her current husband Mark is just that gullible. Unfortunately for her, before she can get away with half the diamonds, the Feds bust her. On the plus side, she's got more than enough dirt on her diamond smuggler husband to make a deal. Mary is understandably disgusted when she's told she has to help this man get a fresh start. She's even angrier when Treena gets engaged to one of the wealthiest men in Albuquerque, allows her engagement photo to be put in the paper (a WITZED no-no), and refuses to call off the wedding despite Mary's warnings.

programme.title: Leverage: Redemption
programme.desc: The team have to infiltrate an e-sports tournament to stop a ruthless team owner from mentally and physically abusing his young players.

programme.title: Covert Affairs
programme.desc: Annie is back on the grid and ready to jump in to work. Though Calder and Auggie suspect that there's more Annie isn't telling them about her absence, the CIA has no choice but to send her back into the field.

programme.title: Truck Night In America
programme.desc: This program makes history with the toughest truck obstacle course ever built. America and trucks go together and, since 1925 when the first American factory-produced truck rolled off the line, American owners have been devising ingenious ways to make them faster, stronger, and better. Five truck owners will compete in a series of gruelling challenges that test their craftsmanship and driving ability to the max. Each episode features unique challenges that test trucks in a way that's never been seen before. The challenges culminate in The Green Hell, a three-mile obstacle course which includes challenges that send trucks flying off jumps, over a mountain of crushed cars and even through a snake-infested swamp. Helping them along the way are four experts in the off-road world - they coach, they build, they fix, but the one thing they can't do is drive. In the end, the truck who finishes is crowned the champion - if they can make it that far. In this episode, Truck Night goes back to the '90s, featuring a line-up of rigs from the millennium's last decade. An emotional story motivates one driver, and a razor-thin margin separates the two finalists when they face off in the Green Hell.

programme.title: You Can't Ask That: Canada
programme.desc: Little People are a highly visible group who face a great deal of social stigma and curiosity. Little people from different backgrounds explain what it is like to live in a world that sees them as 'disabled'.

programme.title: 101 Places to Party Before You Die
programme.desc: Where else can you get a smash burger, ride in a tuktuk and find spiritual LPs? Turns out it's Richmond, Virginia.

programme.title: Wyatt Cenac's Problem Areas
programme.sub-title: Energy Problems, Millennial Problems, Community Policing Problems
programme.desc: Wyatt tries to make coal miners go solar, move millennials into battleships, and understand community policing.

programme.title: Aussie Pickers
programme.desc: Join Lucas Callaghan and Adam McDonald, Australia's own pickers, as they scour the country's back roads and barnyards in the quest to turn one man's trash into another man's treasure. Along the way we meet some of Australia's most authentic and colourful characters, whose stories reveal a rich Australian history, and what the pickers find will surprise and astound you.

programme.title: My Adventures With Superman
programme.sub-title: Adventures Of A Normal Man Pt. 1
programme.desc: Secret alien Clark and Jimmy start their internship at The Daily Planet and meet fellow intern Lois Lane - who ropes them into investigating stolen military robots threatening Metropolis!

programme.title: Todd McFarlane's Spawn
programme.desc: Twitch is assigned to the Spawn case. In his research, Twitch confronts the Spawn in the alley as well as the reporter Lisa Wu. Unfortunately his exploits do not go unnoticed.

programme.title: Todd McFarlane's Spawn
programme.desc: After Cog informs Spawn of his shroud's powers, Spawn transforms himself into human form in order to visit Wanda. Twitch, recovering in the hospital, finds his memory foggy. Terry and Richard Sullivan try to expose Wynn's evil CIA dealings.

programme.title: Big Easy Motors
programme.desc: From a 1957 Chevy Bel Air convertible, to a 1967 Shelby Mustang GT, Charles Handler turns piles of junk into custom cars. He searches all over Louisiana, including the backwoods and the bayou, for classic cars no one wants, invests his own money to fix them up and restore them, and then sells them to the highest bidder. Their cars can sell for upwards of $50,000, so there's a huge profit to be made. But then again, if they put in the work and the car doesn't sell for what they expect it to, they could come home with no profit at all.

programme.desc: All hell breaks loose when paranormal investigators disturb tortured spirits trapped in an old passenger train with a tragic past. programme.title: Road to Riches
programme.desc: It's always amazing what people collect - and Shaun is quite the collector! Among his Pacific artefacts and bizarre art pieces, he has a full size stuffed brown bear. Previously a football club mascot, Shaun picked it up cheap from a fire sale of office furniture and now wants to see if Lucas can give it a good home. Shaun also has a handful of paintings in his garage that he picked up at a garage sale - $50 for 50 paintings. One of these catch Lucas' eye - and turns out to be a genuine Arthur Streeton watercolour. Lucas meets Ian, a collector-come-author who love Australian history, and hanging on his wall is an iconic Ned Kelly movie poster that if authenticated and found to be original could be worth thousands! Lucas is invited to the country to help close a mystery. A landlord tells him that one of his tenants has disappeared without a trace, leaving him to dispose of a house full of collectables!

Hannah's on high alert making sure Sandy's friends are taken care of and Sandy's toast is butter free. Meanwhile, Joao continues to struggle to motivate Jack. Colin wonders if this new crew might be a little too bawdy for him, and Chef Mila's redemption charter gets off to a choppy start forcing Sandy to think outside the box with a unique plan.

programme.title: Love It or List It
programme.desc: Gray loves their home because of the large lot and abundance of privacy. However, April is ready to move closer to town to a home that functions for their family of four. April dislikes the dated fixtures and finishes, the cut-off kitchen and how dated their master bathroom is. Despite their clear desire to have a perfect space, neither can easily decide on design choices. April does not see the merit in spending the time to renovate because there are just too many projects to tackle. programme.desc: High school sweethearts Sharnee and Albert purchased a starter home near their beloved bike trail. While Albert loves the location and his five-minute commute to work, Sharnee would like to pack up and move. With a live-in cousin, their space is limited and Sharnee has nowhere to study or work on her art and is desperate for more space. Can this couple love their home with designer Hilary's help, or will real estate agent David find them a house with all the space they need, close to a bike trail?

programme.title: Carnivale
programme.desc: Shells are flying and weary medics attempt to help an array of maimed and screaming soldiers, one who has lost his legs and an arm - until Ben finally wakes up from this terrible dream. Pulling the sheets off his body, he sees that his arm and legs, too, have become bloody stumps - until, he finally wakes from his dream of waking. Sophie delights Jonesy by bringing over a couple of baseball gloves, and the two enjoy a catch and conversation, as in happier days. But when the conversation turns to Sophie's mother, Jonesy fails to defend Sophie's new friend Libby, and the pleasant afternoon turns ugly. Samson collars Ben and sends him on a mission: he's to recruit a freak with claws for hands, known in the area as 'Scorpion Boy'. Samson gives Ben directions and $15 to bribe the boy's parents if necessary. Brother Justin, meanwhile, is in a mental institution, receiving round after round of radical - and painful - therapies, involving icy submersion, insulin injections and rubber tubes.

Begging her mother to stop screaming in her head, Sophie holds her. When morning comes, Lodz is disturbed at Ben's self-imposed insomnia. "The boy doesn't sleep, he doesn't dream. programme.title: Carnivale
programme.desc: Brother Justin stands in front of his altar, handing out the Eucharist, but instead of the holy wafer, he places a razor blade on each worshipper's tongue. "Body of Christ," he says, as blood drips from their mouths and they swallow. Ben Hawkins reaches the front of the line, but when Justin offers the blade, he seizes the minister's arm. "Body of Christ," Justin says, as the two struggle. "No, it ain't," Ben says. And then, Ben awakes. Nearby, Libby and Sophie are passing the time drinking the local mescal. They talk about magic and Sophie's mother, and a certain sexual tension develops between them. As their hands touch around the bottle, Sophie has a vision of the two of them in bed together, marred by a bloody gash across her hand.

Trouble is, nobody can crack the code used by callers to cover their calls; Bell teaches D'Angelo how to 'school' his lookouts, at the same time flushing out a possible snitch in the low rises; Carver and Herc finally track down Bodie, but find him equally elusive under interrogation.

programme.title: The Wire
programme.desc: With Rawls looking to make a premature murder arrest in three murders linked to D'Angelo and Barksdale, McNulty and Greggs turn to an unlikely ally to secure a delay in the case and preserve the significant wiretap gains being made: Daniels; while Wallace and D'Angelo struggle with their consciences after receiving 'blood money' from Avon, Omar decides to turn witness after seeing his lover Brandon's mutilated body; Bubbles detours from the (not quite) straight and narrow to join fellow junkies Johnny and Hucklebuck in a crime caper; having lost the war to get immediate search warrants, Rawls redoubles his efforts to screw McNulty.

programme.title: The White Lotus
programme.desc: After admitting their marriage needs work, Ethan grows suspicious of Harper and Cam.

programme.title: Euphoria
programme.desc: Kat starts camming; Jules falls for a boy online; Rue is confronted at NA; Cassie visits McKay at college.

programme.title: Euphoria
programme.desc: Rue, trying to get clean for Jules, chaperones Gia at the carnival; Jules encounters Cal with his family; Cassie and Maddy decide to do molly together.

programme.title: The Righteous Gemstones
programme.sub-title: They Are Weak, But He Is Strong
programme.desc: Aimee-Leigh's brother Baby Billy returns to the Gemstone ministry, bringing along his much younger wife Tiffany. Jesse closes ranks on his co-conspirators to root out the identity of his blackmailers.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Ned looks to a book for clues to the death of his predecessor, and uncovers one of King Robert's bastards. Jon takes measures to protect Samwell from further abuse at Castle Black and a frustrated Viserys clashes with Daenerys in Vaes Dothrak. Sansa imagines her future as a queen, while Arya envisions a far different future. Catelyn rallies her husband's allies to make a point, while Tyrion finds himself caught in the wrong place at the wrong time.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Incensed over news of Daenerys' alliance with the Dothrakis, Robert orders a pre-emptive strike on the Targaryens that drives a wedge in his relationship with Ned. A captive Tyrion helps Catelyn, but receives a cold reception at the Eyrie from her sister, John Arryn's widow Lysa.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.sub-title: Ye Dinna Get Used To It
programme.desc: The truth about Lord John Grey's mysterious disappearance is revealed; Brianna faces off with the foes threatening her family.

programme.title: Laid
programme.desc: Ruby attends in-person therapy for the first time in years; when her therapist suggests she take a break from romance, it proves to be more of a challenge than anticipated.

programme.title: Laid
programme.desc: Ruby reconnects with the "one who got away," who gives her more insight into how she is in relationships; AJ makes a discovery that changes the course of the case.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Ned looks to a book for clues to the death of his predecessor, and uncovers one of King Robert's bastards. programme.title: Sex and the City
programme.desc: Charlotte started out the year with what seemed like mission impossible: finding a husband by year's end. Now things with Trey are looking very promising.

programme.title: Sex and the City
programme.desc: While he continues to try and find an apartment in New York City's overpriced real estate market, Steve takes up residency on Miranda's couch.

programme.title: True Blood
programme.desc: In the Season 5 finale Eric embarks on a final, desperate mission to overthrow the Authority and save Bill from losing his humanity. Andy faces the consequences of a Light Pact he made and Alcide readies for a second showdown with J.D. Sam and Luna test their limits in trying to escape the Authority.

programme.title: Carnivale
programme.sub-title: The Day That Was The Day
programme.desc: Gabriel waits in swirling mist for Ben, who finally pulls up in the truck. Ben tells him to put Ruthie in bed and not to let anyone in - even Samson, and Gabriel sits on the steps of the trailer in vigil. Lodz is waiting with Lila in their trailer. Eventually there is a knock at the door and Ben enters, saying he needs help. Ruthie is dead. A smug looking Lodz says he can't help, but he knows someone who can.

programme.title: New Nurses
programme.desc: Peter struggles to work up the nerve to ask Lis out.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard meets a potential investor, while Gilfoyle butts heads with HR. Gavin explores a leaner Hooli's future.

programme.title: Insecure
programme.desc: A sexually frustrated Issa explores a new dating outlook. On a girls' night out, Molly meets a promising new guy. Lawrence's commitment is tested by his new work friends.

programme.title: Insecure
programme.desc: At a day party, Issa has her new-found confidence shaken. Molly has a blast from the past with childhood friend, Dro. A trip to the grocery store turns into a wild and crazy night for Lawrence.

programme.title: Sex and the City
programme.desc: Samantha throws a self-congratulatory soiree at her new apartment and tries to convince the girls that they have it all: great friends, good jobs, and plenty of sex.

programme.title: Sex and the City
programme.desc: As Carrie's affair with Big goes into its third week, the scene turns from ultra-sexy to slightly seedy. In search of some chastising, Carrie turns to the one friend she knows will lay down the law: Miranda.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: As Tony and Carmela struggle with AJ, who is diagnosed with attention deficit disorder, they wonder what the children know about the family business, and Tony remembers his own childhood revelations.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Federal indictments loom, worrying Tony and the family. Christopher's erratic behaviour disturbs Tony who confronts his nephew.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ziva goes into hiding after she witnesses a political assassination and learns that the FBI thinks she is responsible. Led by Tony, the NCIS team look to prove Ziva's innocence.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is forced to come out of retirement to help Fornell, who is being threatened by an ex-sailor who escaped from prison. The investigation will lead the NCIS team to a surprising discovery.

programme.desc: A shootout leaves two cops and a teenage neo-Nazi dead, with lots of unanswered questions as to what happened and who else might have been involved.

programme.title: From The Ashes
programme.desc: The uplifting and remarkable tale of a summer that changed England forever. July 1981, rioting and discontent rule the streets of Britain. Meanwhile, on the playing fields, England are 1-0 down after two tests against the old enemy, Australia; and Ian Botham, their iconic all-rounder, has resigned as English captain, moments before being pushed.

programme.title: G.O.A.T.s with Photographer Walter Iooss
programme.desc: Celebrate the greatest athletes of all time through the extraordinary work of America's most iconic sports photographer, Walter Iooss.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons writer's room is the stuff of TV legend. It would churn out hilarious scripts like a Ford automobile assembly line, turning what could have been a niche, off-beat miss into a surprise hit. The phenomenal success of the show has almost always been attributed to the irreverent and groundbreaking writing, but while the writers toiled away, tensions rose among the three creators.

programme.title: Stars of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: Nicolas Kim Coppola was born January 7, 1964 and known professionally as Nicolas Cage. He is best known for his performance in The Rock (1996), Con Air (1997) and City of Angels (1998).

programme.desc: Did Napster destroy the album, but ultimately save the music industry? Who really killed Keith Moon's chauffeur? Who's responsible for Who Let the Dogs Out?

It would churn out hilarious scripts like a Ford automobile assembly line, turning what could have been a niche, off-beat miss into a surprise hit. The phenomenal success of the show has almost always been attributed to the irreverent and groundbreaking writing, but while the writers toiled away, tensions rose among the three creators.

programme.title: Stars of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: Nicolas Kim Coppola was born January 7, 1964 and known professionally as Nicolas Cage. He is best known for his performance in The Rock (1996), Con Air (1997) and City of Angels (1998).

programme.title: The Brief History Of Cricket Downunder
programme.desc: Cricket thrives on partnerships, showcasing how two players can achieve greater success together than alone.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael gets up close with a piece of natural history, visits a garden used as a viewing platform for public hangings, and experiences a timepiece like no other.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo takes to the sea with the heroes of the Royal National Lifeboat Institution, visits a stormy coastal railway, and has a close personal encounter with his boyhood hero.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Just as critical to the success of the show as the writers, the animators had drama and tension of their own. The funky, punk rock animation house Klasky Csupo, led by Gabor Csupo had secured the contract of a lifetime, but could they hold on to The Simpsons? Through internal strife and natural disasters, the animators gave The Simpsons their signature look.

programme.title: Stars of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: Jeremy Irons was born 19 September 1948 and is an English actor and activist. His first major film role came in the romantic drama The French Lieutenant's Woman (1981), for which he received a BAFTA nomination for Best Actor.

programme.title: History's Greatest Heists
programme.desc: Since the creation of currency, money has made the world go round and people have done anything and everything in their power to get their hands on a lot of it, including formulating some of the most devious and high-stakes heist attempts of all time. Using dramatic recreations, dynamic storytelling and cutting-edge visual effects, alongside first-person witness accounts from the people who were there, the series delves into the intricate schemes and audacity of the criminal masterminds who risked their freedom for a shot at a lifetime of wealth and riches. Brosnan, who is embedded into each heist through state-of-the-art technology, brings each global news headline to life by putting viewers at the heart of the action and breaking down every aspect of the plan including the conniving team, the mark, the execution and finally the aftermath.

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.desc: We follow the winner's progress as they head to Holland to research their 10,000 pound commission for the Queen's House at Royal Museums Greenwich.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Celeste, Lydia West, Alastair Campbell: The competition continues as the artists create portraits and present them to the judges.

programme.desc: The story of Elon Musk's long and often troubled relationship with Twitter, charting his journey from one of the site's most provocative users to its sole proprietor.

programme.title: Europe's Waterways
programme.sub-title: Holland And Belgium - From Rotterdam To The Belgian Ardennes
programme.desc: Venturing upstream on Europe's waterways, the series discovers five regions of the continent from an unknown perspective and visits people who live from, on and with water. Rivers, canals, lake regions: they have shaped Europe's cultures for centuries - they are a place to live, transport arteries and sources of energy. Five journeys on watercourses broad and narrow lead to Italy, Germany, France, Poland, Holland and Belgium. Along the way: new projects for the future of water in Europe.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: The creative minds behind some of George Michael's most memorable music videos reveal how they brought his hits to life on screen.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: The story of how a little-known singer-songwriter from Paddington named Declan McManus became the world-famous Elvis Costello through his powerful introspective lyrics and innovative music videos.

programme.title: Too Young To Die
programme.desc: On August 9, 1969, 26-year-old Sharon and her unborn child are stabbed to death 16 times by a Satanist sect led by its self-appointed guru, Charles Manson.

programme.title: The Canary Islands
programme.sub-title: Tenerife - In The Realm Of Fire
programme.desc: Off the African coast in the middle of the Atlantic, underwater volcanoes raged for millions of years and created a volcanic archipelago: the Canary Islands. Each of them has its own face. This series tells of natural spectacles, unique natural areas and the formation of the volcanic archipelago.

programme.title: World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: A luxurious 500 mile African train safari where we'll be travelling on Rovos Rail's vintage hotel train. Lovingly re-built by the Rovos family to the highest of specifications, this beautiful 14 carriage train leaves Pretoria for its three-day journey to Durban on the coast.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton sets out to experience island life today. He uncovers the past and reveals its connections with the present.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton sets out to experience island life today. He uncovers the past and reveals its connections with the present.

programme.title: The Brief History Of Cricket Downunder
programme.desc: Cricket thrives on partnerships, showcasing how two players can achieve greater success together than alone.

programme.title: Paul Kelly: Stories of me
programme.desc: This film charts the remarkable life of one of Australia's most gifted and beloved singer-songwriters. For almost 40 years, in over 350 songs, Paul Kelly has been mapping out the Australian landscape and its people. For the first time in a feature length film, the man behind the music is revealed, as Kelly speaks candidly about the people who have helped shape his life and music. Featuring rare archival footage, never-before-seen live performances, interviews with Kelly's family members, former and current band members, music contemporaries and many more.

programme.title: Bernadette Peters: Live In Australia
programme.desc: Broadway star Bernadette Peters headlines the 2009 Adelaide Cabaret Festival. From her opening number, Let Me Entertain You, Peters brings wit, emotion and her inimitable style to a range of musical numbers and pop classics. Highlights include a rendition of Fever from atop a grand piano, a heart-rending Rose's Turn (from Gypsy) and a performance of her self-penned charity single Kramer's Song.

programme.title: Branson
programme.desc: The episode chronicles Virgin Atlantic's battle with British Airways, ending in a libel suit win for Branson. From outsider to wealthy capitalist, Branson continues to push for world records and promotional attention for his company, setting off on a nail-biting Pacific Ocean balloon voyage. He announces plans to develop a spaceflight company to take commercial passengers into space, beginning a decade long pursuit.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: Beginning with the Eurythmics' Sweet Dreams and finishing with Walking on Broken Glass, we see the different approach Annie takes with each video.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: A look at the making of the iconic music videos of Paul Weller, the 'Modfather', whose solo hits include The Changingman and You Do Something to Me.

programme.title: Too Young To Die
programme.desc: In November 1981, at the age of 43, Natalie Wood drowns in a tragic accident - a death in which the circumstances have never been clarified.

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael revisits Tibet and Yunnan in China, travelling through eastern India arriving in the Himalayan Kingdom of Bhutan before ending the series in Bangladesh cruising down the Ganges into the Bay of Bengal.

programme.title: World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The Chihuahua Pacific Railway, known as the EL Chepe in northern Mexico, took a hundred years to build. From Creel to the coast, it travels through the spectacular Copper Canyon region.

programme.title: The Jinx: Life & Deaths Of Robert Durst
programme.desc: In the 33 years since Kathie Durst disappeared, no progress has been made in finding out what happened to her, or finding her body. Without answers, her family and friends continue to hunt for the truth.

programme.title: Life Of Crime 1984-2020
programme.desc: The culmination of 36 years of work from multiple Emmy Award-winning producer/director Jon Alpert. Follow Rob, Freddie, and Deliris on a relentless ride through the streets and prisons of Newark, whose lives have been defined by and torn apart by their addictions. With unfettered access, the documentary bears witness to each of their journeys in and out of prison, rehab and in occasional jobs, as they struggle to end the vicious cycles of drug use and to connect with the families they left behind.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: With the departure of the original creative team, a fresh team of writers take over running the show. Bill Oakley and Josh Weinstein build on the legacy, expanding the world and exploring the side characters. To stay relevant, the Simpsons had to evolve; opening its doors to new creative voices, celebrity guest stars and even take on the big screen.

programme.title: Stars of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: Dame Emma Thompson is a British actress, screenwriter, producer, activist, author, and comedian. One of Britain's most acclaimed actresses, she is the recipient of numerous accolades, including two Academy Awards and two Golden Globe Awards.

programme.title: Riveted: The History of Jeans
programme.desc: The history of jeans, from their roots in slavery to its connection to the Wild West, youth culture, rock and roll, high fashion and hip-hop, jeans are the canvas on which the history of American ideology and politics is writ large.

programme.title: Brandy Hellville: Cult of Fast Fashion
programme.desc: BRANDY MELVILLE will expose the teen fashion brand's toxic work culture of discrimination against race, sex, and size. Following the recent breaking story uncovering the truths of the Brandy Melville brand, from former and current executives to employees who are ready to speak out on camera and detail their past traumatic experiences. Brandy Hell-Ville: Cult of Fast Fashion will reveal the inner workings of a corrupt and unethical brand and how it has influenced beauty ideals in the United States and abroad.

programme.title: Inside the Shein Machine
programme.desc: Iman Amrani explores how Shein mastered social media marketing to become a global fast fashion phenomenon. As rumours spread of exploitative working conditions, hidden cameras go inside factories for the first time.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Remaining on the air for over 30 years is no small task yet the Simpsons has always managed to stay fresh and keeping up with the modern era. Even contract negotiations and corporate mergers never put the Simpsons' future into question. But what there's no doubt about is the impact the show has had on viewers, television and popular culture.

programme.title: Stars of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: John Travolta is an American actor, singer, and prominent Scientologist. He rose to fame during the 1970s and starred in the box office hits Carrie (1976), Saturday Night Fever (1977), Grease (1978) and Pulp Fiction (1994).

programme.title: Diana: The Ultimate Truth
programme.desc: More than two decades on, the circumstances around Princess Diana's death continue to create international headlines. A global icon, arguably the most famous woman in the world, killed aged just 36, in a car crash in a Paris underpass. Despite a verdict of unlawful killing and two police investigations into the devastating crash, many questions around what happened, and who is responsible, remain.

programme.title: The Royals Revealed

programme.title: Not So Pretty
programme.desc: The first-ever comprehensive large-scale investigative expose of the trillion-dollar cosmetics, beauty and personal care industry. Celebrated and ground-breaking filmmakers Kirby Dick and Amy Ziering (On The Record, The Hunting Ground, The Invisible War) will take this monolithic industry to task via rigorous investigations, incisive wit and emotional storytelling to inform audiences of the hidden hazards of and safe, budget-friendly alternatives for their daily products.

programme.desc: Thanks to Audrey and Grevil Hartley, Richard has managed to retain his business, but he must raise capital to keep it going.

programme.title: High Plains Drifter
programme.desc: A gunfighting stranger comes to the small settlement of Lago and is hired to bring the townsfolk together in an attempt to hold off three outlaws who are on their way.

programme.title: Strange Bedfellows
programme.desc: An international oil company executive has to watch his corporate image when he is about to divorce his turbulent Italian wife of seven years.

programme.title: The Kid from Texas
programme.desc: Billy the Kid becomes embroiled in Lincoln County, New Mexico, land wars. When the rancher who gave him a break is killed by rival henchman, Billy vows revenge.

programme.title: Maverick
programme.desc: Bart is mistaken for a notorious criminal and is put in prison.

programme.title: Get Smart
programme.desc: Max and new agent Samuelson go undercover as ping-pong champions while trying to learn about a new spy agency called ACB.

programme.title: Get Smart
programme.desc: Max tries to convert a children's book writer into a KAOS agent's wife to get hold of the important Krispin Papers.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Howard falls ill and needs his tonsils removed, and with his employee and Chuck unavailable, Richie is pressed into working at Cunningham's Hardware on Saturday, typically the store's busiest day. Richie agrees, even though he may miss a huge rock concert, and in the process, takes responsibility when a glass pane in the front door of the store is accidentally broken.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Richie and Potsie, in their never-ending effort to get girls, decide that they want to join a gang called the Demons. However, in order to be accepted, they have to perform several dares.

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica moonlights as campaign speechwriter for her friend Kathleen Lane, only to become involved in clearing Kathleen of the murder of her campaign manager.

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Pulled into the middle of an assassination plot that lands her in jail, Jessica is charged as an impostor guilty of murdering a Bulgarian operative.

programme.title: The Wild Wild West
programme.sub-title: The Night Of The Avaricious Actuary
programme.desc: When vibrations from a giant tuning fork destroy the palatial homes of extremely wealthy businessmen, Jim and Artie are called in to investigate.

programme.title: Maverick
programme.desc: Bart gets himself insured after a man insists he pay a debt.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: After taking a look at a B+ Potsie got on an exam, Fonzie decides to drop back into school. However, he asks Richie to do his homework and cheat for him.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Richie and Potsie obtain fake identification cards in order to attend a burlesque show where Ralph says Bubbles McCall takes it all off.

programme.title: The Black Adder
programme.desc: The death penalty hangs over Edmund when a mysterious witch hunter accuses him of being Satan's bedfellow.

programme.title: Get Smart
programme.desc: Max arrives at a bus station locker and receives his orders from a self-destructing tape recorder, a la Mission: Impossible. The tape recorder explodes, destroying everything but the tape.

programme.title: The Brady Bunch
programme.desc: Mike takes the entire family on a trip to the King's Island amusement park, where he is to submit plans to a client, but a mix-up causes the blueprints to go missing at the park.

programme.title: The Brady Bunch
programme.desc: A series of misunderstandings lead the Bradys to believe that Alice and Sam are up to much more than just bowling on their upcoming Saturday night date.

programme.desc: Hogan kidnaps Burkhalter's sister so he can exchange her for a captured Allied agent.

programme.desc: Klink is to be executed when the Gestapo finds him with information Hogan planted on him to deliver to the underground.

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: A senatorial hopeful is murdered and his wife's necklace stolen.

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica is perplexed by the apparent murder of an elderly man pointing towards two spinster sisters he was living with.

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Life as an urban farmer begins to get Barbara down, particularly when a passerby mistakes her for a boy. When Barbara rips her last good dress, she reaches her limit.

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: With everyone watching a football match, Fletch attempts to snatch a few precious minutes of peace and quiet. Meanwhile, Officer Mackay shows the cell block to members of the Home Office.

programme.desc: The Emmy-winning quiz show features a unique answer-and-question format. Dave Bond vs. Bill McKinney vs. Lisa O'Donnell.

programme.title: Rawhide
programme.desc: When a delusional old man attacks the passing drovers, his daughter-in-law enlists Gil & Rowdy's help with the powerful ex-boxing champ. The Marshal who made the town safe, is now drunk on his own power, enforcing petty rules to keep the tiniest semblance of trouble out of his jurisdiction. Factoring heavily into the situation is that the daughter-in-law is exquisite and widowed.

programme.title: Get Smart
programme.desc: Weary of waiting for Max, 99 agrees to marry the casino boss, Victor Royal.

programme.title: Gomer Pyle
programme.desc: Duke draws on Gomer's arm. Lou-Ann thinks it's a real tattoo; she hates it but doesn't tell Gomer thinking it's too late. Gomer hates it but goes to make it permanent thinking Lou-Ann likes it.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: It seems like a bad fit when Yale-educated Roger Phillips is hired over street savvy Fonzie as the new principal of the George S Patton Vocational High School, known for it's rampant juvenile delinquency.

programme.title: McHale's Navy
programme.desc: McHale's men meet Jerry Colonna in New Caledonia, and the famous World War II troubadour promises to come to Taratupa and put on a show. Arrangements hit a snag. It develops that Colonna's tour is limited to bases having at least a 50-bed hospital. As Taratupa's hospital has only 28, McHale's men take steps. Teleplay was written by William Raynor and Myles Wilder. Directed by Sidney Miller.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan goes undercover as a German officer to discredit a German officer who knows about the Stalag 13 operation.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes

programme.title: Are You Being Served?

programme.title: The Presidio
programme.desc: Lt. Col. Caldwell and Police Inspector Jay Austin don't like each other. Caldwell helped run Austin out of the military police a few years back. Now these natural enemies are forced to team up to solve a brutal murder at The Presidio military compound. To make matters worse, Inspector Austin has taken a sudden and passionate interest in a beautiful, feisty young woman - Lt. Col. Caldwell's daughter. Sparks fly between the young lovers as fists and bullets start flying as this action-packed thriller kicks into high gear and tears up the streets of San Francisco.

Binghamton's plan backfires when McHale and his men are reported killed in an engagement with the enemy. Teleplay was written by Sam Locke and Joel Rapp. Directed by Jean Yarbrough.

programme.title: McHale's Navy
programme.desc: Publicity-hungry movie star, Mimi St Clair, comes to Taratupa and secretly plans to be marooned on an island with McHale and his men. Captain Binghamton sends McHale and his men to reconnoitre a deserted island while he tries to enhance his prestige by shoving off Mimi at an admiral's party. But Mimi stows away on PT-73. Teleplay was written by Ray Brenner and Barry E. Blitzer. Directed by Hollingsworth Morse.

programme.desc: J.C. (Malik Yoba) infiltrates a prison to try to bust a drug lord whose release is imminent; Eddie (Michael DeLorenzo) finally proposes to Nina (Lauren Velez). Musical performance by Tracy Chapman.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.
programme.desc: The team is under pressure responding to the shooting of a little girl. Atwater stands up for himself. Voight questions whether he's cut out for a new form of policing.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.
programme.desc: An investigation becomes personal for Voight when an acquaintance from his past is caught putting others' lives in danger.

programme.title: Chicago Fire
programme.desc: Casey and Dawson apply for permanent adoption of Louie. Otis convinces Herrmann to throw a bash for the 100th anniversary of Molly's, but issues arise.

programme.title: Transplant
programme.desc: As shakeups force major turmoil at the hospital, Bash and Amira take a trip.

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A stressed out stay-at-home mother of four boys is so depressed she blows up their car, but she survives, along with her oldest child, and responsibility for the murders is questioned.

programme.title: New York Undercover
programme.desc: J.C. (Malik Yoba) infiltrates a prison to try to bust a drug lord whose release is imminent; Eddie (Michael DeLorenzo) finally proposes to Nina (Lauren Velez). Musical performance by Tracy Chapman.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: When a student's school reports a girl missing, the squad must track down a trusted family friend for answers.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Officer Nate Kendall aids a woman in trouble and becomes roped into solving a rape case.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.
programme.desc: An investigation becomes personal for Voight when an acquaintance from his past is caught putting others' lives in danger.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: The red double-decker bus is a global icon. Now, Gregg Wallace has special access to a factory in Scarborough, Yorkshire where they build this famous people mover.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off
programme.desc: The bakers must fulfil Matt's mantra that patisserie is perfection by piling pastries on the judges' plates.

programme.title: Food Unwrapped Specials
programme.desc: It's Food Unwrapped's Healthy New Year. This special episode is stuffed with virtuous food hacks and little-known science secrets to help turbo-charge your health and well-being. Jimmy discovers if chia seeds could be the key to curbing overeating. Briony asks why producers of gluten free products are seemingly charging more for less. Matt lets the dogs out and discover whether raw pet food could be the key to a healthy pooch. And Kate discovers some surprising science about the watermelon's superpowers.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Together
programme.desc: Jamie takes us through the feast step by step, which has an epic centrepiece at its heart; a slow-roasted, rolled roast shoulder of lamb with a fennel, sage and spicy sausage stuffing.

programme.title: Masterchef UK: Celebrity
programme.desc: The three remaining celebrities must cook a faultless two-course meal that will not only be judged by John and Gregg, but also by three Celebrity MasterChef legends.

programme.title: Taste of the Country
programme.desc: Julius is taking us on an emotional and fun journey as he heads into the woods beside the farmhouse to forage for cherries just like his beloved Granny showed him when he was a small boy.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon concludes with an episode showcasing home cooked food that's incredibly easy to cook but sensational enough to serve for any special occasion. Gordon's son Jack helps him cook an unforgettable dinner of beef fillet with salsa verde.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: Jamie whips up grilled steak, ratatouille, and saffron rice, plus blackened chicken, and a San Fran quinoa salad.

programme.title: Mary Berry's Love To Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry loves to explore new ideas that inspire her to make stand-out dishes that really tantalise the tastebuds.

programme.title: Cook Clever, Waste Less with Prue & Rupy
programme.desc: Vikkie and Wayne in London have a bad habit of cooking a nightly back-up meal for their fussy kids. It's making their shopping bills double the national average and causing their food waste to sky-rocket.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood: A Baker's Life
programme.desc: Paul looks back at his early career, from an apprentice baker to working at a five-star stately home. On the menu: fresh fruit doughnuts, pork pie and Danish pastries.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less
programme.desc: Chris and Gregg meet the Hoyland family. Dad Derek, who is coeliac, hasn't eaten the same meal as his family for nearly six years. Mum, Clair, is brand-obsessed, filling the trolley with frozen convenience food.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood Eats Japan
programme.desc: Paul is now heading out to travel more widely across Japan by road, rail and air. On the road, he happens across a world record breaking bakery. On the railway, he lives out his ultimate train spotter fantasy rides the Shinkansen or bullet train as we know it and experiences the best railway buffet food on earth. And a short flight takes him to the sub tropical islands of Okinawa, once celebrated as the healthiest place in the world, but now becoming addicted to American style fast food. Paul also visits two of Japan's best known cities. First he visits Japan's ancient capital Kyoto where, as well as trying his hand at being a Ninja, meat lover Paul attempts to enjoy tofu in its many forms. Then he moves on to Hiroshima. Whilst most of the world only knows this city as the place they dropped the first atomic bomb, these days the city has a growing reputation across Japan for its food. Paul visits possibly the smallest restaurant in Hiroshima to meet its owner, a bomb survivor who continues to cook and serve the city's signature dish. In what Paul fondly remembers as one of his happiest and favourite days in Japan, she teaches Paul to cook the famous Hiroshima-style Okonimayaki. Finally, Paul travels way up north into the snow to take part in a particularly brutal rice festival. It's a contest where the north and south of the town fight it out with 6 metre long bamboo sticks to see whether they'll get a good rice harvest. Paul gets stuck into to both the fighting and the sake!

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Bakers compete in pastry challenges: frangipane tart Signature, flaky Technical, and choux Paris-Brest Showstopper requiring freestanding podium.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon concludes with an episode showcasing home cooked food that's incredibly easy to cook but sensational enough to serve for any special occasion. Gordon's son Jack helps him cook an unforgettable dinner of beef fillet with salsa verde.

programme.desc: Mary Berry loves to explore new ideas that inspire her to make stand-out dishes that really tantalise the tastebuds.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: This week we have two proudly single mums who've joined forces to rise their kids, start businesses and save money but somehow, they're not saving and family meals end up being the quickest, and most processed, fix available. Enter chef Michael Van de Elzen and restauranteur Ganesh Raj who are going help take the stress - and sugar - out of the family meals and truly help them join forces to stay on budget.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Nastassja attempts an edible baby's mobile suspended 4 and a half feet off the ground for a little boy's first birthday. Sculpted from chocolate brownie cake and rice cereal and marshmallow mix, this cloud themed hanging cake has cloud and giraffe shaped cookies dangling from it. Rosie's also facing a first birthday challenge as she makes a cake replica of a beloved pet to celebrate TV presenter Lorraine Kelly's dog Angus's special day.

programme.title: Chopped Next Gen
programme.desc: Four young, hip and hungry chefs face scorpion powder in round one.

programme.title: MasterChef UK
programme.desc: Tonight, they face just one intense challenge. They must cook two courses that will excite not just John and Gregg, but also some of MasterChef's most inspiring winners, finalists and contestants, who have all gone on to careers in the food industry.

programme.desc: This week's family are American expats excited to rise their kids the 'Kiwi way' but first, they need to understand how seasonal produce works in New Zealand and with several serious health issues they also need a little help to eat their way to wellness, which is where chef Michael Van de Elzen and restauranteur Ganesh Raj come in!

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four of Wales' best chefs compete to stay in the competition over canapes, starters and fish dishes celebrating British animation and illustration for Paddington's 65th birthday.

programme.desc: 'The more the merrier' is a motto Mary Berry lives by. What is better than cooking crowd-pleasing delights and meeting others who also adore feeding the masses?

programme.desc: Gino arrives in Matera James Bond style and explores the cave town where he samples his father's favourite drink Amaro Lucano and Nun's Breast cakes.

programme.desc: Dame Mary Berry knows a thing or two about cooking up a Fantastic Feast, so she's sharing her cooking skills with 3 novice cooks - fireman Mark, youth worker Callum and boxing instructor Thaer who want to throw a spectacular afternoon tea to say thank you to Soraya, who runs a local youth charity. With the help of Dame Mary, can they pull it off and give Soraya a thank you surprise she'll never forget?

programme.desc: NHS doctors Preeti and Sanjeev in Manchester are struggling to juggle their hectic work lives and the multiple meal demands of their three children.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood: A Baker's Life
programme.desc: Paul heads for Cyprus, where he lived for six years. He's reunited with friends and makes a classic ham and cheese croissant, baklava, spanakopita, and a Cypriot village bread.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less
programme.desc: Gregg and Chris meet the King Family. Juggling work and a young daughter is not easy, so Sheena's go-to is pre-packed convenience food but she also ends up cooking two separate dishes every mealtime.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: We get exclusive access to some of the largest food factories in Britain to reveal the secrets behind food production on an epic scale. We look at Britain's love of chocolate.

programme.desc: Jo Brand narrates the story of the very cold war between our beloved ice cream brands, from the early innovations of Wall's, to the sophistication of Mars, and the high-end luxury of Haagen-Dazs.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Lancashire baker Molly, hopes to make a splash with her next cake as she's asked to bake a cake replica of a very special carp- the prize catch of angler Andrew, as a surprise for his 50th birthday. Wedding cake specialist Suzanne, takes a break from the norm as she's asked to make a 30th birthday cake for a modern art fan, who wants an edible installation as a centrepiece for his party. Built from 3 spherical tiers stacked on top of each other at precarious angles, Suzanne battles to get her ganache and fondant coverings smooth enough for the high gloss finish she needs to make this cake a success.

programme.desc: This episode is Bread Week and the bakers must create a focaccia for the Signature Challenge. In the Technical Challenge, they tackle Montreal bagels, while the Showstopper has the bakers creating a sweet bread centrepiece.

programme.title: Jeremy Pang's Asian Kitchen
programme.desc: Jeremy's dishes this week are all comfort food, starting with a meal from his childhood - Guangxi Steamed Chicken topped with chives.

programme.title: French Gourmet with Justine Schofield
programme.desc: Join Justine in the French countryside, savouring mini croissants, exploring the world-famous Chablis wine at Domaine Laroche, and indulging in iconic madeleines.

programme.title: French Gourmet with Justine Schofield
programme.desc: Justine relishes award-winning goat cheese at La Ferme d'Ulteria, crafts a fresh salad, and collaborates with Chef Leo for the Captain's Dinner in the historic Dordogne region.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's My Swedish Kitchen
programme.desc: Rachel picks kale from the west coast of Sweden, and continues to the only large-scale tomato farm in Sweden. In her cabin, she makes a wonderful dish with butternut squash waffles and a mixed cherry tomato salad with crispy bacon.

programme.title: Legends Of The Fork
programme.desc: Buddy visits two generational legacies: family owned and operated Patsy's Italian Restaurant in New York City, and the famed Oaxacan joint in Los Angeles, Guelaguetza.

programme.title: Farm to Fork
programme.desc: Andy is joined by Mark Lebrooy as they collaborate to create a flavoursome dish of crispy skin pork belly, expertly slow-cooked in Master stock and served with a delectable three-flavour sauce.

programme.title: Farm to Fork
programme.desc: Michael is joined by Andy in the kitchen as they craft a delicious miso eggplant dish that tantalises the tastebuds. Next, Andy prepares a barbecue whole snapper.

programme.title: Paddock To Plate
programme.desc: Matt Moran explores the Riverland Region, hoping to snag a Murray Cod and release it back into the wild. He then heads inland to taste local almonds, with Australia now the second largest producer of almonds in the world. Organic citrus is his next discovery, followed by a hunting expedition for duck.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Bakers compete in pastry challenges: frangipane tart Signature, flaky Technical, and choux Paris-Brest Showstopper requiring freestanding podium.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine With Me
programme.desc: In Essex, three rival duos battle it out to host the best dinner party and win a 1000-pounds prize.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine With Me
programme.desc: First to host are Scottish tea room owner Melanie and her Irish husband David, who've blended together both their heritages with a Celtic fusion menu.

programme.title: Jamie's One Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie makes three one-pan delights: speedy smoked salmon pasta, a loaded beef burger with peppers and pickles, and soft sticky toffee apple buns.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Sunday Lunch
programme.desc: Tom cooks a tasty posh version of chicken and chips. He serves each guest their own poussin, served with wedges and two salads, ranch and panzanella.

programme.title: Jamie's Fish Supper
programme.desc: Jamie cooks his favourite fish dishes, perfect for any summer supper.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: Rick visits Berlin, where history and the avant-garde dwell enticingly side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall teems now with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs.

programme.title: James Martin's Home Comforts
programme.desc: Focusing on homegrown and seasonal produce, James takes a crop of fresh British flavours and transports them into recipes to inspire gardeners and home cooks alike. He is joined by Kate Garraway.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands To Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined in Northern Ireland by his good pal and local boy Paul Rankin. They brave the Carrick-a-Rede rope bridge, then James cooks a tasty fish dish with the dramatic coastline as his backdrop.

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: James Martin's American Adventure continues with a cruise along California's Pacific Coast. James rides his motorcycle through the coastal city of Santa Cruz, taking in the fertile nature of the local farmland along the way. James shops at a local farmers' market before cooking his own recipes on a family run artichoke farm. But James couldn't leave before trying his hand at surfing, only to be upstaged by a surfing dog! James also uses the area's rich farming heritage as inspiration to cook California style back at home in the UK.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: This week, the hairy bikers are in god's county, Yorkshire and more especially the Yorkshire coast. The plan is simple, riding through this beautiful location and visiting top food producers who not only have amazing products but equally amazing stories to tell.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Bakers compete in pastry challenges: frangipane tart Signature, flaky Technical, and choux Paris-Brest Showstopper requiring freestanding podium.

programme.title: How Do They Really Do It
programme.desc: This episode goes inside Primark to reveal how a small Irish department store became Britain's biggest clothing retailer, doubling their number of stores in the last 15 years, while the rest of the high street struggles. We go inside to find out the secrets of their success, from the buying team in Dublin HQ, to the world's biggest Primark in Birmingham. We discover how their unique approach to advertising gets them plenty of publicity without spending on TV ads. We see how their stores are carefully designed and curated to get customers in through the door and spending freely. We hear how Primark keeps their prices lower than the competition, and find out how their make up stands up against the big brands. And we hear exactly how Primark keeps their prices so low.

programme.title: River Cottage Summer Collection
programme.desc: Intrepid chef Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall brings a back-to-nature attitude to modern cooking and consumption. As his mission encompasses real people with real food issues, the host takes viewers into homes around Britain to show ordinary folks how easy and enjoyable it can be to move from pre-processed foods to nutritious natural products.

programme.title: River Cottage: Summer's Here
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall returns to River Cottage HQ to explore and celebrate the very best food that summer has to offer, including a lesson in sheep sheering and veal recipes.

programme.title: Beautiful Baking With Juliet Sear
programme.desc: With Christmas fast approaching Juliet turns her attention to baking with children. She makes a festive reindeer cake, a gingerbread house cake, a candy cane cake and snowmen cakes.

programme.title: River Cottage to the Core
programme.desc: Hugh decides to tackle some country and kitchen skills he has never undertaken before. It's all hands to the pump as he milks 48 ewes and sets out to catch his first sea trout, before creating a picnic for the local pub's sports day.

programme.title: Food Unwrapped Specials
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty and Briony May Williams explore Nordic food, from cod farming in Norway to Swedish meatballs and Finnish cuisine, revealing how these dishes shape global tastes and happiness.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace follows 27 tonnes of potatoes from a farm in Hampshire through the largest crisp factory on Earth, as they are peeled, sliced and fried to make more than 5 million packets of crisps every 24 hours.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Bakers compete in pastry challenges: frangipane tart Signature, flaky Technical, and choux Paris-Brest Showstopper requiring freestanding podium.

programme.title: The Great Kiwi Bake Off
programme.desc: The bakers who remain are to undertake a difficult biscuit week. The signature bake presents an unusual challenge as the judges ask the bakers to make a savoury biscuit.

programme.title: Jamie's Fish Supper
programme.desc: Jamie cooks his favourite fish dishes, perfect for any summer supper.

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: Raymond Blanc celebrates cheese in all its forms with Baked Camembert and Homemade Goat Cheese with Chicory and Walnut Salad. His old friend Claude Bosi joins him for a delicious meal.

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking and Carry On
programme.desc: In unprecedented times, Jamie shares delicious recipes that use whatever ingredients you have at hand.

programme.title: River Cottage to the Core
programme.desc: Hugh decides to tackle some country and kitchen skills he has never undertaken before. It's all hands to the pump as he milks 48 ewes and sets out to catch his first sea trout, before creating a picnic for the local pub's sports day.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace follows 27 tonnes of potatoes from a farm in Hampshire through the largest crisp factory on Earth, as they are peeled, sliced and fried to make more than 5 million packets of crisps every 24 hours.

programme.title: Food Unwrapped Specials
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty and Briony May Williams explore Nordic food, from cod farming in Norway to Swedish meatballs and Finnish cuisine, revealing how these dishes shape global tastes and happiness.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood Eats Japan
programme.desc: Paul is now heading out to travel more widely across Japan by road, rail and air. On the road, he happens across a world record breaking bakery. On the railway, he lives out his ultimate train spotter fantasy rides the Shinkansen or bullet train as we know it and experiences the best railway buffet food on earth. And a short flight takes him to the sub tropical islands of Okinawa, once celebrated as the healthiest place in the world, but now becoming addicted to American style fast food. Paul also visits two of Japan's best known cities. First he visits Japan's ancient capital Kyoto where, as well as trying his hand at being a Ninja, meat lover Paul attempts to enjoy tofu in its many forms. Then he moves on to Hiroshima. Whilst most of the world only knows this city as the place they dropped the first atomic bomb, these days the city has a growing reputation across Japan for its food. Paul visits possibly the smallest restaurant in Hiroshima to meet its owner, a bomb survivor who continues to cook and serve the city's signature dish. In what Paul fondly remembers as one of his happiest and favourite days in Japan, she teaches Paul to cook the famous Hiroshima-style Okonimayaki. Finally, Paul travels way up north into the snow to take part in a particularly brutal rice festival. It's a contest where the north and south of the town fight it out with 6 metre long bamboo sticks to see whether they'll get a good rice harvest. Paul gets stuck into to both the fighting and the sake!

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: This lively group of Millennials think of each other as a family, they all agree that the best thing to come out COVID lockdowns was the time they spent together in the kitchen but with each flatmate returning to work, their love of fast food and quick-fix meals has snuck back in. Chef Michael Van de Elzen and restauranteur Ganesh Raj are going to set some challenges for this busy household all in the hope of helping them to 'adult' better!

With the help of Dame Mary, can they pull it off and give Mark a birthday surprise he'll never forget?

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: Under pressure to land a suspect in the Dora Lange murder, Quesada warns Hart and Cohle they might be replaced by three detectives from a new occult-crimes task force. The pair lobbies for extra time in order to follow up on a lead that takes them from a rural cathouse to an incinerated church. With his marriage to Maggie already strained by work, Hart ends up finding respite outside of home.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: A hidden image at the burned-out church leads Cohle and Hart to Joel Theriot, a tent-revival minister whose parishioners finger a scarred "tall man" seen with Dora Lange. Brought in for interrogation, a sex offender matching the description is quickly dismissed by Cohle, despite offering a confession. Hart gets side tracked after Maggie sets Cohle up with a friend at a C&W bar. Looking to establish a pattern of murder, Cohle pores over old case files, finding a connection with a woman assumed to have drowned years earlier.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: Hart and Cohle hunt for their newly identified suspect, Reggie Ledoux, a meth cooker who shared a cell with Dora Lange's husband and recently skipped parole. As Hart's personal life collapses around him, Cohle immerses himself in an old criminal identity from his narco days, contacting an East-Texas biker gang known to deal with their primary suspect.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.sub-title: The Secret Fate Of All Life
programme.desc: A violent denouement in the forest clears the Dora Lange case and turns Cohle and Hart into local heroes. Each man settles into a healthier rhythm of living as Hart returns to his family, and Cohle starts a relationship while gaining a reputation as a closer in interrogations. As time passes and his daughters grow older, Hart faces new tensions and temptations, and Cohle learns from a double-murder suspect that there could be much more to an old case than he'd once thought.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: In 2002, Cohle and Hart begin to fall back to familiar and violent obsessions. Hart exacts savage vengeance on a pair of teenage boys, and Cohle becomes convinced they left something undone in 1995. Working on his own, Cohle traces a sinister connection between missing children along the coast and evangelist Billy Lee Tuttle's Wellsprings Program.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: After Marty is shown a harrowing piece of evidence, he and Cohle forge a truce after years of vitriol, and we learn why Cohle returned to the state in 2010. Hart helps Cohle probe a murky conspiracy of disappearances connected to the Dora Lange murder, a path of connections that leads to the Tuttle family. Hart reconnects with Maggie after years without communication in order to confirm what the police wanted, and perhaps to say goodbye. Concerned that harm might come to him, Maggie pays Cohle a visit at the bar where he's working, but gets a cold shoulder for her efforts. Cohle and Hart uncover a connection to an old colleague and enact a plan to draw him out.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: After their interrogation of Geraci yields new info but no critical breaks, Hart and Cohle find themselves back at square one. However, a seemingly arbitrary detail in a photograph, along with a homeowner's old tax records, provides the pair with a vital new lead in their 17-year-old case. The two men pursue the lead to an apocalyptic confrontation.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.sub-title: The Western Book Of The Dead
programme.desc: The disappearance of a city manager disrupts a lucrative land scheme and ignites an investigation involving three police officers and a career criminal who is moving into legitimate business.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: Ray and Ani investigate a victim's depraved background, as does Frank, while Paul probes the Vinci PD.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: Paul works the prostitute angle while Frank receives the first casualty in a secret war, and steps back into a world he'd left behind.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: The detail works a pawn-shop lead to close in on a suspect in the Caspere case. Meanwhile, Frank revisits his past to pay for his present.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: Ray and Frank contemplate new life choices while Ani and Paul follow a lead up the coast.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: Desperate to locate a missing woman with intel about Caspere, Frank meets with Mexican drug dealers while Ani infiltrates an exclusive inner circle, with Ray and Paul keeping close tabs.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: Ray, Ani and Paul take precautionary measures to elude detection and untangle a dark mystery. Meanwhile, Frank deals with the fallout of his betrayal.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: Frank, Ray and Ani weigh their options as Caspere's killer and the scope of corruption is revealed.

programme.title: True Detective

programme.title: True Detective
programme.sub-title: The Great War And Modern Memory
programme.desc: The disappearance of a young Arkansas boy and his sister in 1980 triggers vivid memories and enduring questions for retired detective Wayne Hays, who worked the case 35 years ago with his then partner Roland West. What started as a routine case becomes a long journey to dissect the crime and make sense of it.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: Hays looks back at the aftermath of the 1980 Purcell case in West Finger, AR, including possible evidence left behind at the Devil's Den, an outdoor hangout for local kids. As attention focuses on two conspicuous suspects; Brett Woodard, a solitary vet and trash collector, and Ted LaGrange, an ex-con with a penchant for children, the parents of the missing kids, Tom and Lucy Purcell, receive a cryptic note from an anonymous source.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: Hays recalls his early romance with Amelia, as well as some cracks in their relationship that surfaced after they married and had children. Ten years after the Purcell crimes took place, new evidence emerges, giving Hays a second chance to vindicate himself and the investigation.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: Hays and West see a possible connection between the local church and the Purcell crimes. As the detectives search for one suspect and round up another one for interrogation, Woodard finds himself targeted by a vigilante group.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: Wayne finds himself in a no-win situation as new clues emerge in the Purcell case. Roland wrestles with how to keep evidence secure as lawyers demand a new investigation. Amelia finds her relationship with Wayne imperiled by her writing aspirations and his jealousy.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: Wayne and Roland revisit discrepancies in the Purcell case that have been hidden or forgotten over the years. Among those being reevaluated is Tom Purcell, as well as Lucy Purcell's cousin Dan O'Brien. The glitter of Amelia's book release is tarnished by a voice from the past.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: Following up on new leads, Wayne and Roland track down a man who left the police force in the midst of the Purcell investigation. Meanwhile, Amelia visits Lucy Purcell's best friend in hopes of gaining insights into the whereabouts of the mysterious one-eyed man.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: Wayne struggles to hold on to his memories, and his grip on reality, as the truth behind the Purcell case is finally revealed.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: In Ennis, Alaska, the men who operate a research station vanish. To solve the case, Detectives Danvers and Navarro must confront the darkness themselves, and dig into the haunted truths that lie buried.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: As Danvers and Prior set out to learn more about Tsalal and find an unlikely location for the physical evidence, Captain Connelly threatens to move the case to Anchorage.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: While Hank leads the search for Clark, Prior asks Danvers about the murder-suicide case that drove a wedge between her and Navarro.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: When Julia's mental health struggles resurface, Navarro brings her to a local facility, promising this time will be better.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: As Prior digs into the links between Tsalal and mining company Silver Sky, Navarro rescues Leah from a local protest that has turned violent.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: Amid a brutal storm, Danvers and Navarro find themselves stuck at Tsalal. As the truth about what happened to Annie and the Tsalal men unfolds, Navarro and Danvers confront the demons from their past.

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: Following up on new leads, Wayne and Roland track down a man who left the police force in the midst of the Purcell investigation. Meanwhile, Amelia visits Lucy Purcell's best friend in hopes of gaining insights into the whereabouts of the mysterious one-eyed man.

programme.title: Chernobyl
programme.desc: April 26, 1986, Ukrainian SSR. Plant workers and firefighters put their lives on the line to control a catastrophic explosion at a Soviet nuclear power plant.

programme.title: Chernobyl
programme.desc: With untold millions at risk after the Chernobyl explosion, nuclear physicist Ulana Khomyuk makes a desperate attempt to reach Valery Legasov, a leading Soviet nuclear physicist, and warn him about the threat of second explosion that could devastate the continent.

programme.title: Chernobyl
programme.desc: Lyudmilla Ignatenko, a Pripyat resident, ignores warnings about her firefighter husband's contamination; Valery Legasov lays out a decontamination plan, complete with human risks.

programme.title: Chernobyl
programme.desc: Valery Legasov and Soviet Deputy Prime Minister Boris Shcherbina consider using lunar rovers to remove radioactive debris, while Ulana Khomyuk faces government hurdles in determining the truth about the cause of the explosion.

programme.title: Chernobyl
programme.desc: Valery Legasov, Boris Shcherbina and Ulana Khomyuk risk their lives and reputations to expose the truth about Chernobyl.

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: Five young graduates do whatever it takes to make themselves indispensable at pre-eminent London investment bank Pierpoint & Co.

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: With Harper struggling to find her feet in a new city, Pierpoint throws a morale-boosting appreciation dinner which sees her challenging a drunken Kenny; Gus alienates senior management.

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: When Pierpoint loses a major client, the grads face their most challenging and potentially rewarding test to-date: devising pitches to drive new business.

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: After a wild birthday celebration with Robert, Yasmin, and Greg, Harper's workday spirals out of control. As a discrepancy in one of her trades puts her team at risk for major losses, Harper seeks surprising support.

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: As Pierpoint is shaken by a former employee's scathing expose, Eric goes off-site for the day, prompting an unauthorised Gus to cover trades from his desk.

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: Sex, drugs, and secrets light up Pierpoint's annual holiday party, where Maxim's unexpected arrival aggravates tensions between Yasmin and Kenny, and Greg finds himself in a painful predicament.

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: With Reduction in Force Day looming, the graduates do whatever it takes to secure final recommendations from their superiors and solidify vital client relationships.

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: After a tumultuous few months at Pierpoint, the graduates take vastly different approaches as they stand before senior management to prove they're worthy of permanent positions.
